SEARCH
Package edu.bu.pas.risk.agent

Class NeuralQAgent

java.lang.Object
edu.bu.pas.risk.agent.FSMAgent
edu.bu.pas.risk.agent.NeuralQAgent
All Implemented Interfaces:
IAgent, RiskListener, Cloneable
public abstract class NeuralQAgent extends FSMAgent

Represents a agent containing a neural network acting as a q-function. The neural network we're using in this class is a DualDecoderModel. This model requires a GameView be turned into a feature vector, an Action turned into a feature vector and a Territory turned into a feature vector. These conversions are carried out by SensorArray objects: one sub-class per type of conversion. For instance the StateSensorArray type is responsible for converting states (e.g. GameView) to feature vectors.

This class also stores reward functions (RewardFunction objects) so we know how to punish/pleasure the model according to its behavior. There are two decision points: choosing an Action and a Territory so there are two RewardFunction objects: one per decision point.

Taking actions in this game is complicated (see IAgent for more details). To keep track of which "phase" in this agent's turn there is a state machine that increments depending on what the action q-function selects. See FSMAgent for more details.

Author:
Andrew Wood

    • chooseRedeemAction

      public final Action chooseRedeemAction(GameView game, int actionCounter, boolean canRedeemCards)
      Description copied from class: FSMAgent
      A method to get the Action when you are in the redeeming cards phase of your turn.
      Specified by:
      chooseRedeemAction in class FSMAgent
      Parameters:
      game - The game state.
      actionCounter - How many actions you have taken so far this turn. Starts at 0 always.
      canRedeemCards - If you are allowed to send in a RedeemCardsAction. This is true when you have not started your attack phase, and have enough cards to trade in. This is also true when you have eliminated an opponent and now have 6 or more territory cards.
      Returns:
      The action you choose to do at this point in the game.

    • chooseAttackActionRedeemIfForced

      public final Action chooseAttackActionRedeemIfForced(GameView game, int actionCounter, boolean canRedeemCards)

      canRedeemCards - If you are allowed to send in a RedeemCardsAction. This is true when you have not started your attack phase, and have enough cards to trade in. This is also true when you have eliminated an opponent and now have 6 or more territory cards.
      Returns:

      Description copied from class: FSMAgent
      A method to get the Action when you are in the fortify/skip fortify phase of your turn.
      Specified by:
      chooseFortifySkipAction in class FSMAgent
      Parameters:
      game - The game state.
      actionCounter - How many actions you have taken so far this turn. Starts at 0 always.
      canRedeemCards - If you are allowed to send in a RedeemCardsAction. This is true when you have not started your attack phase, and have enough cards to trade in. This is also true when you have eliminated an opponent and now have 6 or more territory cards.
      Returns:
      The action you choose to do at this point in the game.

    • placeOneArmy

      public final Territory placeOneArmy(GameView game, boolean isDuringSetup, int remainingArmies)
      During initial board setup, agents get to choose which territory they want. In this method, agents should choose an unclaimed territory (see TerritoryOwnerView.isUnclaimed()) to claim.

      During the setup phase, this method will be called MANY times, until the entire board is claimed. At each call, you are responsible for returning exactly 1 territory.

      The default implementation of this method randomly selects any unclaimed territory. 

      
         List<Territory> unownedTerritories = game.getUnownedTerritories();
         if (!unownedTerritories.isEmpty()) {
             // during the setup phase, we should always try to claim an
             // unclaimed territory. Consider biasing this random to claim
             // "chunks" of territories, or target specific continents.
             int randomIdx = game.getRandom().nextInt(unownedTerritories.size());
             return unownedTerritories.get(randomIdx);
         }

         // otherwise, just pick a territory we own at random
         List<Territory> ownedTerritories = game.getTerritoriesOwnedBy(this.agentId());
         int randomIdx = game.getRandom().nextInt(ownedTerritories.size());
         return ownedTerritories.get(randomIdx);
      Parameters:
      game - The current game state.
      isDuringSetup - true if this is at the START of the game.
      remainingArmies - How many armies you have left to place.
      Returns:
      The territory you want to claim.
      See Also:
      • GameView.getUnownedTerritories()
